info.name: Auth – Register (POST /api/v1/auth/register)
info.description:
E2E tests for POST /api/v1/auth/register.

Folder execution order (orchestrated by make e2e-auth):
  1. infrastructure  — health, security headers, content-type guard
  2. validation      — all 422/413 input-error cases
  3. happy-path      — 201 success + boundary cases + indirect DB-state checks
  4. conflict        — 409 duplicate email + indirect state checks
  5. rate-limiting   — 429 after bucket exhaustion (Makefile flushes Redis before this folder)

All test users use @e2e.test emails so `make _e2e-clean` can wipe them with a single DELETE.

Notes on indirect DB-state checks:
  - email_verified=false is verified by attempting POST /login → 403 email_not_verified.
  - is_locked=false is verified by the same login returning 403 NOT 423.
  - Original user survival after a conflict is verified the same way.

DB-state assertions (display_name trimming, token row shape, TTL window, audit rows,
punycode storage) are covered by internal store/validator integration tests and do
not require a separate e2e DB-introspection pass.

item.name: email domain label starts with hyphen (IDNA Lookup rejects) → 422
email ≤ 254 bytes pre-IDNA but > 254 bytes after punycode expansion → 422

exec: pm.request.body.update({ mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ display_name: 'Alice', email: 'bigbody@e2e.test', password: 'P@ssw0rd1!', _pad: pad }) });

item.name: email is lowercased → 201 (UPPER@E2E.TEST stored as lower)
registered user has email_verified=false and is_locked=false (indirect: POST /login → 403 email_not_verified)
original user row unaffected after conflict (indirect: POST /login → 403 email_not_verified)

exec: pm.test('code is email_not_verified (user row intact, password unchanged)', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.json().code).to.eql('email_not_verified'); });

item.name: rate-limiting
item.description: Burst=5 (NewIPRateLimiter rate=5/(10*60) burst=5). The folder-level prerequest overrides _xff to 127.0.0.1 so all 6 requests share one bucket. 5 warmups drain it, the 6th must 429. Requires TRUSTED_PROXIES=127.0.0.1/32 in server .env.

item.name: warmup 5/5 — consume slot (→ not 429, last allowed)
rate-limit-reset
item.description: Verifies that the rate-limit bucket resets after the 10-minute window expires. The Makefile flushes Redis (FLUSHDB via _e2e-kv-clean) before this folder runs, which is functionally equivalent to the window expiring and the token bucket refilling.

item.name: first request after window reset → 201 (bucket refilled)

exec: pm.test('status is 201 (rate-limit window reset confirmed)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

variable.description: Set by the happy-path folder. Reused by the conflict and indirect DB-state check tests.
Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique IP by the collection prerequest; overridden to 127.0.0.1 by the rate-limiting folder prerequest.
Set by the happy-path 'valid username at registration' test. Reused by the conflict folder's username_taken test.
Email of the user registered with a username in the happy-path folder.
